description: Intent-anchored eval scenarios for the google-docs connector arranged on the setup spectrum so a 3-arm run (with_skill / without_skill / native) reveals native's true capability profile. Because the connector can create every document it needs in-run, the corpus is all arrange-act (no fixtures): each scenario self-provisions its world, exercises the capability, and where relevant cleans up. Assertions split into outcome (backend-agnostic world/reply facts graded on all arms) and mechanism (tool-name/efficiency/shape diagnostics, our-arm-only). {{run_id}} namespaces created assets. Run per the agentskills.io eval methodology.

fixtures.name: CANDIDATE_NAME
fixtures.description: Literal placeholder token used in the create-document-from-template scenario; substitutes to itself so the agent sees it verbatim.

fixtures.name: START_DATE
fixtures.description: Literal placeholder token used in the create-document-from-template scenario; substitutes to itself so the agent sees it verbatim.

evals.description: Read-tier coverage probe (the dominant operation): create a uniquely-titled doc, then find it by name via Drive search.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Quarterly Roadmap {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A document titled 'Quarterly Roadmap {{run_id}}' now exists and its id (or link) is reported back.
mechanism: Uses a single create-document call; the document id comes from the create result, not a hard-coded or invented id.

steps.intent: Find the Google Doc whose title is 'Quarterly Roadmap {{run_id}}'.

outcome: The document titled 'Quarterly Roadmap {{run_id}}' is located and a distinguishing detail (its id or link) is returned.
mechanism: Discovers the document by searching on the title text given in the request rather than guessing or hard-coding a document id.

evals.description: Read-tier probe: create a doc with known body text, then read its full contents back.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Team Update {{run_id}}' with the body text: 'We shipped the new dashboard on Monday. Latency dropped by 30 percent. Next we tackle onboarding.'

outcome: A document titled 'Team Update {{run_id}}' exists and its body contains the three sentences given in the request.
mechanism: Creates the document and populates the body with the requested text (the create call carries the text, since a bare create honors only the title); does not claim success without writing the body.

steps.intent: Read back the full contents of the 'Team Update {{run_id}}' document you just created.

outcome: The reply reproduces the document body — the dashboard, latency, and onboarding sentences given in the request — accurately.
mechanism: Reads the document the agent just created in this turn rather than fabricating content; retrieves it via a read/export call instead of replaying the text from memory only.

evals.description: Read-tier probe: create a multi-paragraph doc, then summarize it (a clean-text read is the natural path).

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Incident Review {{run_id}}' with the body: 'At 09:14 the checkout API began returning 500s. The cause was an expired TLS certificate on the payments gateway. The on-call rotated the cert and traffic recovered by 09:41. Follow-up: automate certificate renewal.'

outcome: A document titled 'Incident Review {{run_id}}' exists and its body contains the incident narrative given in the request.
mechanism: Uses a single create-document call carrying the body text; the document id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Fetch the full text of the 'Incident Review {{run_id}}' document.

outcome: The full content of the document is retrieved and presented — it contains the incident narrative about the expired TLS certificate.
mechanism: Calls exportDocument or getDocument to retrieve the document content rather than reciting text from a previous step.

steps.intent: Summarize what the 'Incident Review {{run_id}}' document says.

outcome: The summary covers all three key facts: an expired TLS certificate caused checkout 500s, the cert was rotated to restore traffic, and the follow-up action is to automate certificate renewal. Accept an accurate summary regardless of whether the agent made a tool call — the content accuracy is the test.

evals.description: Read-tier probe: create a doc containing a known phrase, then locate that phrase's position (the index resolver).

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Meeting Agenda {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Welcome and intros. Status updates. Action Items. Open discussion.'

outcome: A document titled 'Meeting Agenda {{run_id}}' exists and its body contains the phrase 'Action Items'.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Find where the phrase 'Action Items' appears in the 'Meeting Agenda {{run_id}}' document.

outcome: The reply reports that 'Action Items' was found in the document and identifies its location (an index range or position).
mechanism: Locates the phrase by searching the document text rather than fabricating an index range; the reported position is derived from a read of the document, not invented.

evals.description: Read-tier probe: create a doc with several headed sections, then read just one named section.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Project Brief {{run_id}}' with three sections: a 'Goals' section reading 'Ship v1 by August.', a 'Budget' section reading 'Allocated 40k for the quarter.', and a 'Risks' section reading 'Vendor lead times are uncertain.'

outcome: A document titled 'Project Brief {{run_id}}' exists containing distinct Goals, Budget, and Risks sections with the text given in the request.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested multi-section body; the document id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: What does the 'Budget' section of the 'Project Brief {{run_id}}' document say?

outcome: The reply returns the Budget section's content ('Allocated 40k for the quarter.') and does not return the Goals or Risks content as the answer.
mechanism: Reads the document and isolates the Budget section rather than guessing; the returned text comes from a read of the document just created.

evals.description: Create-tier probe: create a new empty document with a given title.

steps.intent: Create a new, empty Google Doc titled 'Weekly Notes {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A document titled 'Weekly Notes {{run_id}}' now exists and its id or link is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single create-document call; the title matches the request exactly and the id comes from the create result, not a hard-coded id.

evals.description: Create-tier probe: create a document seeded with plain body text in one request.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Standup {{run_id}}' that contains the text: 'Yesterday: finished the migration. Today: write tests. Blockers: none.'

outcome: A document titled 'Standup {{run_id}}' exists and its body contains the yesterday/today/blockers text given in the request.
mechanism: Creates the document and writes the requested body text (passed on the create call, since a bare create honors only the title); does not report success without the body present.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: create a document from Markdown so headings/bold/lists render as real document formatting rather than literal Markdown characters.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Release Notes {{run_id}}' from this Markdown:

# Release 2.0

This release is **major**. Highlights:

- Faster sync
- New export formats

See the _migration guide_ for details.

outcome: The created document renders the Markdown as real formatting — 'Release 2.0' is a styled heading, 'major' is bold, and 'Faster sync'/'New export formats' appear as list items — not as literal '#', '**', or '-' characters in the body.
mechanism: Renders the content via the connector's Markdown-aware create path (treats the input as Markdown) rather than inserting the raw Markdown source as plain text.

evals.description: Edit-tier probe: create a doc, then append a paragraph to the end without disturbing existing content.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Newsletter {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Thanks for reading this month.'

outcome: A document titled 'Newsletter {{run_id}}' exists and its body contains 'Thanks for reading this month.'
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Append a closing line to the end of the 'Newsletter {{run_id}}' document: 'See you next month — the team.'

outcome: The text 'See you next month — the team.' was appended to the document.
mechanism: Uses appendText (not insertText with a computed index) to add the text to the end of the document.

evals.description: Edit-tier probe: create a doc, then insert text at a resolved position (lookup-before-write, index ≥ 1).

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Recipe {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Ingredients
Steps'

outcome: A document titled 'Recipe {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the words 'Ingredients' and 'Steps'.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Recipe {{run_id}}' document, insert the line '2 cups flour' on its own immediately after the word 'Ingredients'.

outcome: The text '2 cups flour' now appears immediately after 'Ingredients' and before 'Steps' in the document.
mechanism: Resolves the insertion position by reading the document (locating 'Ingredients') before inserting, rather than guessing an index; the insertion index is at least 1 (not the reserved index 0).

evals.description: Edit-tier probe: create a doc with a repeated term, then find-and-replace every occurrence.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Spec {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Project Apollo kicks off in May. Project Apollo has three milestones. Project Apollo ships in Q4.'

outcome: A document titled 'Spec {{run_id}}' exists and its body contains the phrase 'Project Apollo' more than once.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Spec {{run_id}}' document, rename every mention of 'Project Apollo' to 'Project Mercury'.

outcome: No occurrence of 'Project Apollo' remains in the document and 'Project Mercury' now appears in each place it was.
The reply reports how many occurrences were changed.
mechanism: Uses a find-and-replace-all operation keyed on the exact text given in the request and surfaces the occurrences-changed count rather than assuming the replacement happened.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: a find-and-replace whose search term is absent returns zero changes — the agent must surface the no-op, not claim success.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Changelog {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Added dark mode. Fixed the login bug. Improved sync speed.'

outcome: A document titled 'Changelog {{run_id}}' exists with the changelog body given in the request.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Changelog {{run_id}}' document, replace every occurrence of 'Quokka' with 'Wallaby'.

outcome: The reply communicates that nothing matched — no occurrences were changed — and does not claim that a replacement was successfully made.
The document body is unchanged from the original changelog text.
mechanism: Surfaces the zero occurrences-changed result from the replace operation as a no-op warning instead of reporting a successful edit.

evals.description: Destructive edit: create a doc, then delete one uniquely-tagged sentence — either replaceAllText (pattern match) or findText+deleteContentRange (range-based) are valid paths.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Draft Post {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Our results were strong. DELETE THIS: internal note, do not publish. Thanks for reading.'

outcome: A document titled 'Draft Post {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the 'DELETE THIS:' sentence alongside the surrounding text.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Draft Post {{run_id}}' document, remove the sentence that begins 'DELETE THIS:'.

outcome: The 'DELETE THIS: internal note, do not publish.' sentence is gone, while 'Our results were strong.' and 'Thanks for reading.' remain in the document.
mechanism: Deletes only the target sentence — either by using replaceAllText with the exact text as the find string (empty replace), or by reading the document to resolve its range and calling deleteContentRange.

evals.description: Destructive edit requiring range resolution: the target text appears twice so replaceAllText would over-delete — the agent must read the document, resolve the exact index range of one occurrence, and use deleteContentRange.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Status Report {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Version: 1.0

Version: 1.0

Release date: 2026-07-01.'

outcome: A document titled 'Status Report {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains two 'Version: 1.0' paragraphs and the release date line.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Delete only the second 'Version: 1.0' paragraph from the 'Status Report {{run_id}}' document, leaving the first one intact.

outcome: The document now contains exactly one 'Version: 1.0' paragraph and the 'Release date: 2026-07-01.' paragraph — the first 'Version: 1.0' is preserved.
mechanism: Reads the document to resolve the index range of the second 'Version: 1.0' paragraph, then uses deleteContentRange to remove only that range — does not use replaceAllText (which would remove both occurrences).

evals.description: Destructive edit, second variant (deletion is not single-capped): create a multi-section doc, then delete one entire named section.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Planning Doc {{run_id}}' with two sections: a 'Decisions' section reading 'We will launch in Q3.' and a 'Scratch notes' section reading 'random brainstorm, ignore later.'

outcome: A document titled 'Planning Doc {{run_id}}' exists containing both a 'Decisions' section and a 'Scratch notes' section with the text given in the request.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested two-section body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Delete the entire 'Scratch notes' section from the 'Planning Doc {{run_id}}' document.

outcome: The 'Scratch notes' heading and its 'random brainstorm, ignore later.' body are removed, while the 'Decisions' section and 'We will launch in Q3.' remain intact.
mechanism: Reads the document to resolve the section's start and end before deleting, deletes only that range (not adjacent sections), and avoids removing the segment's trailing newline.

evals.description: Formatting probe: create a doc, then apply character formatting to a resolved range.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Report {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Summary
The quarter exceeded targets.'

outcome: A document titled 'Report {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the word 'Summary'.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Report {{run_id}}' document, make the word 'Summary' bold and set its size to 18 points.

outcome: The word 'Summary' is now bold and rendered at 18 points, while the rest of the body keeps its original formatting.
mechanism: Resolves the range of 'Summary' by reading the document before formatting, and applies both bold and the 18-point size to that range only — not to unrelated text.

evals.description: Template probe: create a document containing placeholder tokens, then produce a new document from it with the placeholders filled.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Offer Template {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Dear {{CANDIDATE_NAME}}, we are delighted to offer you the role starting {{START_DATE}}.'

outcome: A document titled 'Offer Template {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the literal placeholder tokens {{CANDIDATE_NAME}} and {{START_DATE}}.
mechanism: Creates the document with the placeholder text intact in a single create call.

steps.intent: Create a new document titled 'Offer - Ada {{run_id}}' from the 'Offer Template {{run_id}}' template, filling {{CANDIDATE_NAME}} with 'Ada Lovelace' and {{START_DATE}} with '2026-07-01'.

outcome: A new document titled 'Offer - Ada {{run_id}}' was created with both {{CANDIDATE_NAME}} and {{START_DATE}} placeholders replaced (occurrencesChanged ≥ 1 reported for each).
mechanism: Copies the template document the agent just created and applies the placeholder-to-value mapping given in the request; reports the per-placeholder replacement counts so an unmatched placeholder would be visible.

evals.description: Image probe: create a doc, then insert an inline image from a public URL.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Brand Assets {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A document titled 'Brand Assets {{run_id}}' now exists and its id or link is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single create-document call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Insert this image into the 'Brand Assets {{run_id}}' document: https://www.google.com/images/branding/googlelogo/2x/googlelogo_color_272x92dp.png

outcome: An inline image sourced from the given URL is now present in the document, and an object id for the inserted image is reported.
mechanism: Calls insertImage with the public URL given in the request rather than fabricating an image object id.

evals.description: Image probe: create a doc with an image, then swap that image for a different public image (object id resolved from a fresh read).

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Photo Doc {{run_id}}' and insert this image into it: https://www.google.com/images/branding/googlelogo/2x/googlelogo_color_272x92dp.png

outcome: A document titled 'Photo Doc {{run_id}}' exists containing an inline image sourced from the given URL.
mechanism: Creates the document and inserts the image from the public URL given; the inserted image yields an object id from the insert result.

steps.intent: Replace the image in the 'Photo Doc {{run_id}}' document with this one instead: https://www.google.com/images/branding/googleg/1x/googleg_standard_color_128dp.png

outcome: The inline image in the document now displays the replacement image rather than the original logo.
mechanism: Obtains the existing image's object id — either from the insertImage result in the previous step or by reading the document — then calls replaceImage with the new URL rather than inserting a second image.

evals.description: Guidance-dependent: 'one inch' margins must be expressed in points (72), the unit the API uses — the model tends to default to the wrong unit.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Letter {{run_id}}' with the body 'To whom it may concern,'.

outcome: A document titled 'Letter {{run_id}}' exists with the requested opening line.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Set the page margins of the 'Letter {{run_id}}' document to one inch on all four sides.

outcome: The document's top, bottom, left, and right margins are all set to one inch (72 points).
mechanism: Converts one inch to 72 points and sets all four margins in points (the unit the document-style update expects), rather than passing a value of 1 or any non-point unit.

evals.description: Disambiguation: two documents share a title, so an edit-by-name request must ask which one rather than silently picking.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Budget {{run_id}}' with the body 'Version A figures.'

outcome: A document titled 'Budget {{run_id}}' exists containing 'Version A figures.'
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Create a second Google Doc, also titled 'Budget {{run_id}}', with the body 'Version B figures.'

outcome: A second, distinct document also titled 'Budget {{run_id}}' exists containing 'Version B figures.'
mechanism: Creates a new document rather than editing or overwriting the existing same-titled one.

steps.intent: Append the line 'Approved.' to the document titled 'Budget {{run_id}}'.

outcome: Because two documents share the title 'Budget {{run_id}}', the reply reports the ambiguity and asks which document to edit (or lists the candidates with a distinguishing detail) rather than silently editing one or both.
mechanism: Does not perform an append on either same-titled document before the ambiguity is resolved; surfaces the tied candidates with a distinguishing field such as last-modified time or link.

evals.description: Capability: the connector authors tables (insertTable). Create a doc, then add a 3-column by 2-row table.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Inventory {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A document titled 'Inventory {{run_id}}' now exists and its id or link is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single create-document call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add a 3-column by 2-row table to the 'Inventory {{run_id}}' document.

outcome: A real 3-column by 2-row table is added to the document (confirmable by reading it back as a table structural element), not tab- or newline-delimited text pretending to be a table.
mechanism: Uses the table-authoring tool with rows and columns (not a text-insert tool faking a table with delimiters); the reported table start position comes from the tool result, not a guess.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: the connector has no comment-write capability — the agent should say so, not approximate a comment with body text.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Proposal {{run_id}}' with the body 'Here is our proposal.'

outcome: A document titled 'Proposal {{run_id}}' exists with the requested body.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Add a comment saying 'Please review by Friday' to the 'Proposal {{run_id}}' document.

outcome: The reply states that adding comments is not supported and does not claim a comment was added.
The comment text is not silently inserted into the document body as a substitute for a real comment.
mechanism: Does not call any editing tool to fake a comment by writing the comment text into the document body.

evals.description: Graceful refusal: export supports only plain text and Markdown — a PDF request should be declined (with the supported formats offered), not faked.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Invoice {{run_id}}' with the body 'Amount due: 500 dollars.'

outcome: A document titled 'Invoice {{run_id}}' exists with the requested body.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Export the 'Invoice {{run_id}}' document as a PDF file.

outcome: The reply states that PDF export is not supported — only plain text and Markdown are available — and does not claim a PDF was produced.
No fabricated PDF file or download link is presented as the result.
mechanism: Does not invent a PDF or a download URL; if it exports anything it uses the supported plain-text or Markdown format.

evals.description: Lists: create a doc with plain lines, then turn them into a bulleted list.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Grocery List {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Apples
Bread
Milk'

outcome: A document titled 'Grocery List {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the lines Apples, Bread, and Milk.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Grocery List {{run_id}}' document, turn the three lines (Apples, Bread, Milk) into a bulleted list.

outcome: The three lines are now a bulleted list — each of Apples, Bread, and Milk is its own bullet — and their text is unchanged.
mechanism: Resolves the range of the three paragraphs by reading the document, then applies bulleted-list formatting to that range with the list tool — not by prepending '-' or '*' characters as literal text.

evals.description: Lists: create a doc with plain steps, then turn them into a numbered list.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Recipe Steps {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Preheat the oven
Mix the batter
Bake for 30 minutes'

outcome: A document titled 'Recipe Steps {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the three step lines.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Recipe Steps {{run_id}}' document, turn the three step lines into a numbered list.

outcome: The three lines are now a numbered list (1, 2, 3) in order, with their text unchanged.
mechanism: Resolves the paragraph range by reading the document and applies numbered-list formatting via the list tool — not by typing '1.', '2.' as literal text.

evals.description: Lists: nesting — a sub-item indented one level under its parent.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Outline {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Section One
Detail A
Section Two'

outcome: A document titled 'Outline {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains the lines Section One, Detail A, and Section Two.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Outline {{run_id}}' document, make the three lines a bulleted list where 'Detail A' is nested one level under 'Section One', with 'Section One' and 'Section Two' at the top level.

outcome: The document contains a bulleted list in which 'Detail A' is a sub-bullet indented one level beneath 'Section One', while 'Section One' and 'Section Two' are top-level bullets.
mechanism: Applies list formatting with per-paragraph nesting levels via the list tool, rather than faking indentation with spaces or tabs typed as text.

evals.description: Lists: strip bullets from an existing list, keeping the text.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Tasks {{run_id}}' from this Markdown:
- First task
- Second task

outcome: A document titled 'Tasks {{run_id}}' exists containing a bulleted list of 'First task' and 'Second task'.
mechanism: Creates the document from Markdown so the two items render as real bullets, not literal '-' characters.

steps.intent: Remove the bullet formatting from the list in the 'Tasks {{run_id}}' document, leaving the two lines as plain paragraphs.

outcome: The two task lines remain with their text intact but are no longer bulleted — they are plain paragraphs.
mechanism: Resolves the list's range by reading the document and uses the remove-list-formatting tool, rather than deleting and retyping the lines.

evals.description: Paragraph styling: apply a named heading style and alignment to a resolved paragraph.

steps.intent:
Create a Google Doc titled 'Report {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Quarterly Report
Results were strong this quarter.'

outcome: A document titled 'Report {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains 'Quarterly Report' and the results sentence.
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: In the 'Report {{run_id}}' document, make the line 'Quarterly Report' a Heading 1 and center it.

outcome: The line 'Quarterly Report' is now styled as Heading 1 and center-aligned, while the sentence below keeps normal body styling.
mechanism: Resolves the paragraph range by reading the document and applies the named paragraph style (Heading 1) and center alignment via the paragraph-formatting tool — not character formatting such as manual bold or font-size changes.

evals.description: Tables: insert a table and populate its cells in one request.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Inventory {{run_id}}'.

outcome: A document titled 'Inventory {{run_id}}' now exists and its id or link is reported.
mechanism: Uses a single create-document call; the id comes from the create result.

steps.intent: Add a 2-row by 3-column table to the 'Inventory {{run_id}}' document, with the first row reading 'Item', 'Qty', 'Price' and the second row reading 'Apples', '5', '$3'.

outcome: A real 2-row by 3-column table exists in the document, with the first row reading Item / Qty / Price and the second row reading Apples / 5 / $3 (confirmable by reading it back as a table).
mechanism: Uses the table tool to create the 2x3 table and populate its cells with the given values, not tab- or newline-delimited text pretending to be a table.

evals.description: Tables: add and remove rows/columns of an existing table.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Schedule {{run_id}}' and add a 2-row by 2-column table to it.

outcome: A document titled 'Schedule {{run_id}}' exists containing a 2-row by 2-column table.
mechanism: Uses the table tool to create the 2x2 table; the reported table position comes from the tool result.

steps.intent: In the 'Schedule {{run_id}}' document's table, add a new row at the bottom and a new column on the right.

outcome: The table now has 3 rows and 3 columns, with the original cells preserved.
mechanism: Resolves the table's position from a document read and uses the table-modify tool to insert the row and the column, rather than recreating the table.

steps.intent: Now delete the bottom row of that table in the 'Schedule {{run_id}}' document.

outcome: The table now has 2 rows and 3 columns.
mechanism: Uses the table-modify tool to delete the referenced row, not a content-range deletion that would corrupt the table.

evals.description: Headers/footers: create a header and a footer and write text into each segment.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Memo {{run_id}}' with the body: 'Body text.'

outcome: A document titled 'Memo {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains 'Body text.'
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Add a page header to the 'Memo {{run_id}}' document containing the text 'CONFIDENTIAL'.

outcome: The document now has a header whose text is 'CONFIDENTIAL', and the body is unchanged.
mechanism: Creates the header with 'CONFIDENTIAL' supplied as the text parameter in the createHeader call.

steps.intent: Add a footer to the 'Memo {{run_id}}' document containing the text 'Draft - do not distribute'.

outcome: The document now has a footer whose text is 'Draft - do not distribute'.
mechanism: Creates the footer with the text supplied as the text parameter in the createFooter call.

evals.description: Footnotes: attach a footnote to body text and write its content.

steps.intent: Create a Google Doc titled 'Paper {{run_id}}' with the body: 'The earth orbits the sun.'

outcome: A document titled 'Paper {{run_id}}' exists whose body contains 'The earth orbits the sun.'
mechanism: Creates the document with the requested body in a single create call.

steps.intent: Add a footnote to the 'Paper {{run_id}}' document with the note text 'Heliocentric model, Copernicus 1543.'

outcome: The document has a footnote whose content reads 'Heliocentric model, Copernicus 1543.', referenced from the body.
mechanism: Creates the footnote at a body position, then writes the note text into the returned footnote segment — not into the document body.
